senses.gloss: a sound (of a thing, an animal, and so on)
a sound (of a thing, an animal, and so on); the sound of human speech or human singing, a voice

examples.text: فِي الاِنْتِخَابَاتِ، سَيَقُومُ الْمُوَاطِنُونَ بِإِلْقَاءِ صَوْتِهِمْ لِاخْتِيَارِ الْمُرَشِّحِ الْأَنْسَبِ.

senses.gloss: to make a sound, to sound

etymology.text: The sense “vote” is probably a loan translation from a European language, most likely French voix, but compare also German Stimme and stimmen, Russian голос (golos) and голосовать (golosovatʹ), etc.

body.text: [an inf. n. (see 1): and also a simple subst., signifying]
a noise, a voice, a cry, a shout, an exclamation,
(MA, PS;) of a human being and of other things: (ISk, TA:) conventionally, the
considered with regard to the degree of elevation or depression of the voice:] and
] and it is used to signify
: (M, Msb, TA:) it is masc.: (S, * M, Msb, TA:) in the following verse, (S, M, Msb,) of Ruweyshid Ibn-Ketheer (S, M) Et-Tá-ee, (S,)

yaA A^ay~uhaA Alr~aAkibu AlmuzojiY maTiy~atahu
                      
                     
                        saAyilo baniY A^asadK maA h`*ihi AlS~awotu
O thou, the rider urging on his beast, ask the sons of Asad what is this clamour?
], (S, M, Msb,) the poet has made
, (S,) or he has made it fem. as meaning
: (M:) the like is often done by the Arabs, when two words, masc. and fem., are syn.: thus they say,
: (Msb:) but the making a masc. n. fem. for this reason is bad; though the reverse is held to be allowable. (M.) The Arabs say,
voice, but I see not a deed.
in the Kur xvii. 66 is said to mean
A noun significant of a sound:
nouns of this kind being of two classes; namely,
nouns applied to the purpose of addressing irrational beings, or what are virtually in the predicament of irrational beings, as young infants;
the former class consists of two descriptions of words; namely,
ejaculations used for the purpose of chiding,
ejaculations used for the purpose of calling,
(to an ass): of the other class are
(imitative of the cry of the crow) and
(imitative of the sound produced by the falling of stones) and

(imitative of the sound produced by the fall of a sword) &c.: nouns significant of sounds are generally indecl., because they resemble certain particles in neither governing nor being governed; but some of them are occasionally decl. [like other nouns]. (ElAshmoonee's Expos. of the Alfeeyeh of Ibn-Málik, section
.) ―         -b3-  See also the next paragraph, in four places.
